'大阪府四條畷市岡山2丁目7-12', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shinobugaoka-jinja}} [[JA:忍陵神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11490800}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2019年12月}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinobugaoka Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shinobugaoka-jinja, sandou.jpg|250px]]
Approach | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Shijōnawate|Q742160}} Okayama 2-7-12 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}}（small）
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinobugaoka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinobugaoka Shrine''' (しのぶがおかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Okayama, {{jalink|Shijōnawate|Q742160}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. The nearby {{jalink|Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840}} is named after the shrine. It is sometimes read as “Ninryō-jinja.” It stands on the mound of '''{{jalink|Shinobugaoka Kofun|Q11490749}}''', and the site is also said to have held '''Okayama Castle''' in the Sengoku period. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{jalink|Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995}}, with associated deities {{jalink|Kumano Ōkami|Q11568889}}, {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Umamori Ōkami}}, and {{jalink|Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|Taishōgun-no-Kami}}. === History === Shinobugaoka Shrine was renamed in Meiji 44 after Tsuhoko Shrine merged two nearby shrines. Tsuhoko Shrine’s original deities were {{jalink|Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995}}, {{jalink|Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|Kumano Ōkami}}. The founding date of Tsuhoko Shrine is unknown, but it is seen as originally worshiping the ancestral deity of the local ancient clan {{jalink|Tsuhoko clan}}, later taking {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} as ujigami. Until the Edo period it was called Shingū Daigongen, with a tradition that {{jalink|En no Gyōja}} founded it in the time of {{jalink|Emperor Tenmu|Q335181}}. As {{jalink|Kumano worship}} spread in the Heian period, {{jalink|Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|Kumano Ōkami}} was merged in. Its original site was at Akayama in Tsuboi, Okayama village, south of the current site, but in the Edo period it moved to the current site and merged Kamatari-sha (deity: Fujiwara no Kamatari) that had been here. In {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked village shrine of former Okayama village. In Meiji 44, it merged the following two shrines. *Former Suna village shrine, Umamori Shrine (deity {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Umamori Ōkami}}) *Former Suna village unranked shrine, Taishōgun-sha (deity {{jalink|Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|Taishōgun-no-Kami}}) === Precincts === === Shinobugaoka Kofun === {{main|忍岡古墳}} In {{jalink|1934|Q18714}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 9), the shrine buildings collapsed due to the {{jalink|Muroto Typhoon|Q4563431}}. During restoration, a {{jalink|pit-type stone chamber|Q11598499}} of a {{jalink|keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718}} was discovered and named “Shinobugaoka Kofun.” On March 31, {{jalink|1972|Q2476}}, the stone chamber was designated an Osaka Prefecture {{jalink|cultural property|Q858308}}. Shinobugaoka Kofun is a 87 m-long keyhole-shaped tumulus built around the mid-{{jalink|4th century|Q8099}} in the {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}}. === Okayama Castle === Okayama Castle is said to have been built around {{jalink|1560|Q6613}} ({{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} 3), when {{jalink|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Q1064909}} held {{jalink|Iimoriyama Castle|Q11666638}} as his main base, as an auxiliary castle using the ridge of Shinobugaoka Kofun northeast of Iimoriyama. In the Eiroku and {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} eras, the then-castle lord {{jalink|Yūki clan|Q2975399}} were devoted {{jalink|Kirishitan|Q718714}}, and it became a Christian center. In {{jalink|1615|Q6784}} ({{jalink|Keichō|Q1328185}} 20), during the {{jalink|Siege of Osaka#Summer Campaign|Q475780|Summer Campaign of Osaka}}, the site was used as a Tokugawa main camp. After victory it came to be called '''Okachiyama''' (“victory mountain”) as an auspicious place, but most remains were destroyed and little can now be seen. 画像:忍岡古墳 01.jpg|Shinobugaoka Kofun monument 画像:忍陵神社 02.JPG|Old waka monument of Shinobu no Oka === Access === *{{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} ({{jalink|Katamachi Line|Q1073379|Gakkentoshi Line}}) "{{jalink|Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840}}" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shinobugaoka-jinja}} *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai3sibu/sijyounawate-city/m30_06_sijyounawate_ninnryou.html Shinobugaoka Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office{{リンク切れ|date=2022-10}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しのふかおかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:四條畷市の建築物]] [[Category:四條畷市の歴史]] [[Category:藤原鎌足]]

'大阪府四條畷市岡山2丁目7-12', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shinobugaoka-jinja}} [[JA:忍陵神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11490800}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2019年12月}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinobugaoka Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shinobugaoka-jinja, sandou.jpg|250px]]
Approach | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Shijōnawate|Q742160}} Okayama 2-7-12 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}}（small）
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinobugaoka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinobugaoka Shrine''' (しのぶがおかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Okayama, {{jalink|Shijōnawate|Q742160}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. The nearby {{jalink|Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840}} is named after the shrine. It is sometimes read as “Ninryō-jinja.” It stands on the mound of '''{{jalink|Shinobugaoka Kofun|Q11490749}}''', and the site is also said to have held '''Okayama Castle''' in the Sengoku period. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{jalink|Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995}}, with associated deities {{jalink|Kumano Ōkami|Q11568889}}, {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Umamori Ōkami}}, and {{jalink|Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|Taishōgun-no-Kami}}. === History === Shinobugaoka Shrine was renamed in Meiji 44 after Tsuhoko Shrine merged two nearby shrines. Tsuhoko Shrine’s original deities were {{jalink|Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995}}, {{jalink|Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|Kumano Ōkami}}. The founding date of Tsuhoko Shrine is unknown, but it is seen as originally worshiping the ancestral deity of the local ancient clan {{jalink|Tsuhoko clan}}, later taking {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} as ujigami. Until the Edo period it was called Shingū Daigongen, with a tradition that {{jalink|En no Gyōja}} founded it in the time of {{jalink|Emperor Tenmu|Q335181}}. As {{jalink|Kumano worship}} spread in the Heian period, {{jalink|Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|Kumano Ōkami}} was merged in. Its original site was at Akayama in Tsuboi, Okayama village, south of the current site, but in the Edo period it moved to the current site and merged Kamatari-sha (deity: Fujiwara no Kamatari) that had been here. In {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked village shrine of former Okayama village. In Meiji 44, it merged the following two shrines. *Former Suna village shrine, Umamori Shrine (deity {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Umamori Ōkami}}) *Former Suna village unranked shrine, Taishōgun-sha (deity {{jalink|Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|Taishōgun-no-Kami}}) === Precincts === === Shinobugaoka Kofun === {{main|忍岡古墳}} In {{jalink|1934|Q18714}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 9), the shrine buildings collapsed due to the {{jalink|Muroto Typhoon|Q4563431}}. During restoration, a {{jalink|pit-type stone chamber|Q11598499}} of a {{jalink|keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718}} was discovered and named “Shinobugaoka Kofun.” On March 31, {{jalink|1972|Q2476}}, the stone chamber was designated an Osaka Prefecture {{jalink|cultural property|Q858308}}. Shinobugaoka Kofun is a 87 m-long keyhole-shaped tumulus built around the mid-{{jalink|4th century|Q8099}} in the {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}}. === Okayama Castle === Okayama Castle is said to have been built around {{jalink|1560|Q6613}} ({{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} 3), when {{jalink|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Q1064909}} held {{jalink|Iimoriyama Castle|Q11666638}} as his main base, as an auxiliary castle using the ridge of Shinobugaoka Kofun northeast of Iimoriyama. In the Eiroku and {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} eras, the then-castle lord {{jalink|Yūki clan|Q2975399}} were devoted {{jalink|Kirishitan|Q718714}}, and it became a Christian center. In {{jalink|1615|Q6784}} ({{jalink|Keichō|Q1328185}} 20), during the {{jalink|Siege of Osaka#Summer Campaign|Q475780|Summer Campaign of Osaka}}, the site was used as a Tokugawa main camp. After victory it came to be called '''Okachiyama''' (“victory mountain”) as an auspicious place, but most remains were destroyed and little can now be seen. 画像:忍岡古墳 01.jpg|Shinobugaoka Kofun monument 画像:忍陵神社 02.JPG|Old waka monument of Shinobu no Oka === Access === *{{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} ({{jalink|Katamachi Line|Q1073379|Gakkentoshi Line}}) "{{jalink|Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840}}" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shinobugaoka-jinja}} *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai3sibu/sijyounawate-city/m30_06_sijyounawate_ninnryou.html Shinobugaoka Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office{{リンク切れ|date=2022-10}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しのふかおかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:四條畷市の建築物]] [[Category:四條畷市の歴史]] [[Category:藤原鎌足]][[Category:qq]]

'大阪府四條畷市岡山2丁目7-12', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shinobugaoka-jinja}} [[JA:忍陵神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11490800}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2019年12月}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinobugaoka Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shinobugaoka-jinja, sandou.jpg|250px]]
Approach | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Shijōnawate|Q742160}} Okayama 2-7-12 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}}（small）
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | 例祭={{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinobugaoka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinobugaoka Shrine''' (しのぶがおかじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Okayama, {{jalink|Shijōnawate|Q742160}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. The nearby {{jalink|Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840}} is named after the shrine. It is sometimes read as “Ninryō-jinja.” It stands on the mound of '''{{jalink|Shinobugaoka Kofun|Q11490749}}''', and the site is also said to have held '''Okayama Castle''' in the Sengoku period. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{jalink|Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995}}, with associated deities {{jalink|Kumano Ōkami|Q11568889}}, {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Umamori Ōkami}}, and {{jalink|Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|Taishōgun-no-Kami}}. === History === Shinobugaoka Shrine was renamed in Meiji 44 after Tsuhoko Shrine merged two nearby shrines. Tsuhoko Shrine’s original deities were {{jalink|Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995}}, {{jalink|Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|Kumano Ōkami}}. The founding date of Tsuhoko Shrine is unknown, but it is seen as originally worshiping the ancestral deity of the local ancient clan {{jalink|Tsuhoko clan}}, later taking {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} as ujigami. Until the Edo period it was called Shingū Daigongen, with a tradition that {{jalink|En no Gyōja}} founded it in the time of {{jalink|Emperor Tenmu|Q335181}}. As {{jalink|Kumano worship}} spread in the Heian period, {{jalink|Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|Kumano Ōkami}} was merged in. Its original site was at Akayama in Tsuboi, Okayama village, south of the current site, but in the Edo period it moved to the current site and merged Kamatari-sha (deity: Fujiwara no Kamatari) that had been here. In {{jalink|1872|Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked village shrine of former Okayama village. In Meiji 44, it merged the following two shrines. *Former Suna village shrine, Umamori Shrine (deity {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Umamori Ōkami}}) *Former Suna village unranked shrine, Taishōgun-sha (deity {{jalink|Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|Taishōgun-no-Kami}}) === Precincts === === Shinobugaoka Kofun === {{main|忍岡古墳}} In {{jalink|1934|Q18714}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 9), the shrine buildings collapsed due to the {{jalink|Muroto Typhoon|Q4563431}}. During restoration, a {{jalink|pit-type stone chamber|Q11598499}} of a {{jalink|keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718}} was discovered and named “Shinobugaoka Kofun.” On March 31, {{jalink|1972|Q2476}}, the stone chamber was designated an Osaka Prefecture {{jalink|cultural property|Q858308}}. Shinobugaoka Kofun is a 87 m-long keyhole-shaped tumulus built around the mid-{{jalink|4th century|Q8099}} in the {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}}. === Okayama Castle === Okayama Castle is said to have been built around {{jalink|1560|Q6613}} ({{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}} 3), when {{jalink|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Q1064909}} held {{jalink|Iimoriyama Castle|Q11666638}} as his main base, as an auxiliary castle using the ridge of Shinobugaoka Kofun northeast of Iimoriyama. In the Eiroku and {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} eras, the then-castle lord {{jalink|Yūki clan|Q2975399}} were devoted {{jalink|Kirishitan|Q718714}}, and it became a Christian center. In {{jalink|1615|Q6784}} ({{jalink|Keichō|Q1328185}} 20), during the {{jalink|Siege of Osaka#Summer Campaign|Q475780|Summer Campaign of Osaka}}, the site was used as a Tokugawa main camp. After victory it came to be called '''Okachiyama''' (“victory mountain”) as an auspicious place, but most remains were destroyed and little can now be seen. 画像:忍岡古墳 01.jpg|Shinobugaoka Kofun monument 画像:忍陵神社 02.JPG|Old waka monument of Shinobu no Oka === Access === *{{jalink|JR West|Q502125}} ({{jalink|Katamachi Line|Q1073379|Gakkentoshi Line}}) "{{jalink|Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840}}" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shinobugaoka-jinja}} *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai3sibu/sijyounawate-city/m30_06_sijyounawate_ninnryou.html Shinobugaoka Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office{{リンク切れ|date=2022-10}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しのふかおかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:四條畷市の建築物]] [[Category:四條畷市の歴史]] [[Category:藤原鎌足]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'大阪府四條畷市岡山2丁目7-12', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shinobugaoka-jinja}} [[JA:忍陵神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11490800}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2019年12月}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinobugaoka Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shinobugaoka-jinja, sandou.jpg|250px]]
Approach | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Shijōnawate|Q742160|WD=Q742160}} Okayama 2-7-12 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995}} | 社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinobugaoka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinobugaoka Shrine''' (しのぶがおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Okayama, {{ill|lt=Shijōnawate|Q742160|WD=Q742160}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. The nearby {{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840|WD=Q855840}} is named after the shrine. It is sometimes read as “Ninryō-jinja.” It stands on the mound of '''{{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Kofun|Q11490749|WD=Q11490749}}''', and the site is also said to have held '''Okayama Castle''' in the Sengoku period. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995}}, with associated deities {{ill|lt=Kumano Ōkami|Q11568889|WD=Q11568889}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Umamori Ōkami}}, and {{ill|lt=Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|WD=Q167008|lt=Taishōgun-no-Kami}}. === History === Shinobugaoka Shrine was renamed in Meiji 44 after Tsuhoko Shrine merged two nearby shrines. Tsuhoko Shrine’s original deities were {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995}}, {{ill||lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Ōkami}}. The founding date of Tsuhoko Shrine is unknown, but it is seen as originally worshiping the ancestral deity of the local ancient clan {{ill||lt=Tsuhoko clan|UNKNOWN}}, later taking {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} as ujigami. Until the Edo period it was called Shingū Daigongen, with a tradition that {{ill||lt=En no Gyōja|UNKNOWN}} founded it in the time of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}. As {{ill||lt=Kumano worship|UNKNOWN}} spread in the Heian period, {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Ōkami}} was merged in. Its original site was at Akayama in Tsuboi, Okayama village, south of the current site, but in the Edo period it moved to the current site and merged Kamatari-sha (deity: Fujiwara no Kamatari) that had been here. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked village shrine of former Okayama village. In Meiji 44, it merged the following two shrines. *Former Suna village shrine, Umamori Shrine (deity {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Umamori Ōkami}}) *Former Suna village unranked shrine, Taishōgun-sha (deity {{ill|lt=Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|WD=Q167008|lt=Taishōgun-no-Kami}}) === Precincts === === Shinobugaoka Kofun === {{main|忍岡古墳}} In {{ill|lt=1934|Q18714|WD=Q18714}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 9), the shrine buildings collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Muroto Typhoon|Q4563431|WD=Q4563431}}. During restoration, a {{ill|lt=pit-type stone chamber|Q11598499|WD=Q11598499}} of a {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718}} was discovered and named “Shinobugaoka Kofun.” On March 31, {{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}, the stone chamber was designated an Osaka Prefecture {{ill|lt=cultural property|Q858308|WD=Q858308}}. Shinobugaoka Kofun is a 87 m-long keyhole-shaped tumulus built around the mid-{{ill|lt=4th century|Q8099|WD=Q8099}} in the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}}. === Okayama Castle === Okayama Castle is said to have been built around {{ill|lt=1560|Q6613|WD=Q6613}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 3), when {{ill|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Q1064909|WD=Q1064909}} held {{ill|lt=Iimoriyama Castle|Q11666638|WD=Q11666638}} as his main base, as an auxiliary castle using the ridge of Shinobugaoka Kofun northeast of Iimoriyama. In the Eiroku and {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} eras, the then-castle lord {{ill|lt=Yūki clan|Q2975399|WD=Q2975399}} were devoted {{ill|lt=Kirishitan|Q718714|WD=Q718714}}, and it became a Christian center. In {{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 20), during the {{ill|lt=Siege of Osaka#Summer Campaign|Q475780|WD=Q475780|lt=Summer Campaign of Osaka}}, the site was used as a Tokugawa main camp. After victory it came to be called '''Okachiyama''' (“victory mountain”) as an auspicious place, but most remains were destroyed and little can now be seen. 画像:忍岡古墳 01.jpg|Shinobugaoka Kofun monument 画像:忍陵神社 02.JPG|Old waka monument of Shinobu no Oka === Access === *{{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} ({{ill|lt=Katamachi Line|Q1073379|WD=Q1073379|lt=Gakkentoshi Line}}) "{{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840|WD=Q855840}}" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shinobugaoka-jinja}} *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai3sibu/sijyounawate-city/m30_06_sijyounawate_ninnryou.html Shinobugaoka Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office{{リンク切れ|date=2022-10}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しのふかおかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:四條畷市の建築物]] [[Category:四條畷市の歴史]] [[Category:藤原鎌足]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'大阪府四條畷市岡山2丁目7-12', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shinobugaoka-jinja}} [[JA:忍陵神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11490800}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2019年12月}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinobugaoka Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shinobugaoka-jinja, sandou.jpg|250px]]
Approach | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Shijōnawate|Q742160|WD=Q742160}} Okayama 2-7-12 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinobugaoka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinobugaoka Shrine''' (しのぶがおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Okayama, {{ill|lt=Shijōnawate|Q742160|WD=Q742160}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. The nearby {{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840|WD=Q855840}} is named after the shrine. It is sometimes read as “Ninryō-jinja.” It stands on the mound of '''{{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Kofun|Q11490749|WD=Q11490749}}''', and the site is also said to have held '''Okayama Castle''' in the Sengoku period. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995}}, with associated deities {{ill|lt=Kumano Ōkami|Q11568889|WD=Q11568889}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Umamori Ōkami}}, and {{ill|lt=Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|WD=Q167008|lt=Taishōgun-no-Kami}}. === History === Shinobugaoka Shrine was renamed in Meiji 44 after Tsuhoko Shrine merged two nearby shrines. Tsuhoko Shrine’s original deities were {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Ōkami}}. The founding date of Tsuhoko Shrine is unknown, but it is seen as originally worshiping the ancestral deity of the local ancient clan {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsuhoko clan|UNKNOWN}}, later taking {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} as ujigami. Until the Edo period it was called Shingū Daigongen, with a tradition that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=En no Gyōja|UNKNOWN}} founded it in the time of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}. As {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano worship|UNKNOWN}} spread in the Heian period, {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Ōkami}} was merged in. Its original site was at Akayama in Tsuboi, Okayama village, south of the current site, but in the Edo period it moved to the current site and merged Kamatari-sha (deity: Fujiwara no Kamatari) that had been here. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked village shrine of former Okayama village. In Meiji 44, it merged the following two shrines. *Former Suna village shrine, Umamori Shrine (deity {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Umamori Ōkami}}) *Former Suna village unranked shrine, Taishōgun-sha (deity {{ill|lt=Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|WD=Q167008|lt=Taishōgun-no-Kami}}) === Precincts === === Shinobugaoka Kofun === {{main|忍岡古墳}} In {{ill|lt=1934|Q18714|WD=Q18714}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 9), the shrine buildings collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Muroto Typhoon|Q4563431|WD=Q4563431}}. During restoration, a {{ill|lt=pit-type stone chamber|Q11598499|WD=Q11598499}} of a {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718}} was discovered and named “Shinobugaoka Kofun.” On March 31, {{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}, the stone chamber was designated an Osaka Prefecture {{ill|lt=cultural property|Q858308|WD=Q858308}}. Shinobugaoka Kofun is a 87 m-long keyhole-shaped tumulus built around the mid-{{ill|lt=4th century|Q8099|WD=Q8099}} in the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}}. === Okayama Castle === Okayama Castle is said to have been built around {{ill|lt=1560|Q6613|WD=Q6613}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 3), when {{ill|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Q1064909|WD=Q1064909}} held {{ill|lt=Iimoriyama Castle|Q11666638|WD=Q11666638}} as his main base, as an auxiliary castle using the ridge of Shinobugaoka Kofun northeast of Iimoriyama. In the Eiroku and {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} eras, the then-castle lord {{ill|lt=Yūki clan|Q2975399|WD=Q2975399}} were devoted {{ill|lt=Kirishitan|Q718714|WD=Q718714}}, and it became a Christian center. In {{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 20), during the {{ill|lt=Siege of Osaka#Summer Campaign|Q475780|WD=Q475780|lt=Summer Campaign of Osaka}}, the site was used as a Tokugawa main camp. After victory it came to be called '''Okachiyama''' (“victory mountain”) as an auspicious place, but most remains were destroyed and little can now be seen. 画像:忍岡古墳 01.jpg|Shinobugaoka Kofun monument 画像:忍陵神社 02.JPG|Old waka monument of Shinobu no Oka === Access === *{{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} ({{ill|lt=Katamachi Line|Q1073379|WD=Q1073379|lt=Gakkentoshi Line}}) "{{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840|WD=Q855840}}" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shinobugaoka-jinja}} *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai3sibu/sijyounawate-city/m30_06_sijyounawate_ninnryou.html Shinobugaoka Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office{{リンク切れ|date=2022-10}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しのふかおかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:四條畷市の建築物]] [[Category:四條畷市の歴史]] [[Category:藤原鎌足]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'大阪府四條畷市岡山2丁目7-12', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shinobugaoka-jinja}} [[JA:忍陵神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11490800}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2019年12月}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinobugaoka Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shinobugaoka-jinja, sandou.jpg|250px]]
Approach | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Shijōnawate|Q742160|WD=Q742160}} Okayama 2-7-12 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinobugaoka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinobugaoka Shrine''' (しのぶがおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Okayama, {{ill|lt=Shijōnawate|Q742160|WD=Q742160}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. The nearby {{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840|WD=Q855840}} is named after the shrine. It is sometimes read as “Ninryō-jinja.” It stands on the mound of '''{{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Kofun|Q11490749|WD=Q11490749}}''', and the site is also said to have held '''Okayama Castle''' in the Sengoku period. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995}}, with associated deities {{ill|lt=Kumano Ōkami|Q11568889|WD=Q11568889}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Umamori Ōkami}}, and {{ill|lt=Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|WD=Q167008|lt=Taishōgun-no-Kami}}. === History === Shinobugaoka Shrine was renamed in Meiji 44 after Tsuhoko Shrine merged two nearby shrines. Tsuhoko Shrine’s original deities were {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Ōkami}}. The founding date of Tsuhoko Shrine is unknown, but it is seen as originally worshiping the ancestral deity of the local ancient clan {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsuhoko clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, later taking {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} as ujigami. Until the Edo period it was called Shingū Daigongen, with a tradition that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=En no Gyōja|1=UNKNOWN}} founded it in the time of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}. As {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano worship|1=UNKNOWN}} spread in the Heian period, {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Ōkami}} was merged in. Its original site was at Akayama in Tsuboi, Okayama village, south of the current site, but in the Edo period it moved to the current site and merged Kamatari-sha (deity: Fujiwara no Kamatari) that had been here. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked village shrine of former Okayama village. In Meiji 44, it merged the following two shrines. *Former Suna village shrine, Umamori Shrine (deity {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Umamori Ōkami}}) *Former Suna village unranked shrine, Taishōgun-sha (deity {{ill|lt=Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|WD=Q167008|lt=Taishōgun-no-Kami}}) === Precincts === === Shinobugaoka Kofun === {{main|忍岡古墳}} In {{ill|lt=1934|Q18714|WD=Q18714}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 9), the shrine buildings collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Muroto Typhoon|Q4563431|WD=Q4563431}}. During restoration, a {{ill|lt=pit-type stone chamber|Q11598499|WD=Q11598499}} of a {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718}} was discovered and named “Shinobugaoka Kofun.” On March 31, {{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}, the stone chamber was designated an Osaka Prefecture {{ill|lt=cultural property|Q858308|WD=Q858308}}. Shinobugaoka Kofun is a 87 m-long keyhole-shaped tumulus built around the mid-{{ill|lt=4th century|Q8099|WD=Q8099}} in the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}}. === Okayama Castle === Okayama Castle is said to have been built around {{ill|lt=1560|Q6613|WD=Q6613}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 3), when {{ill|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Q1064909|WD=Q1064909}} held {{ill|lt=Iimoriyama Castle|Q11666638|WD=Q11666638}} as his main base, as an auxiliary castle using the ridge of Shinobugaoka Kofun northeast of Iimoriyama. In the Eiroku and {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} eras, the then-castle lord {{ill|lt=Yūki clan|Q2975399|WD=Q2975399}} were devoted {{ill|lt=Kirishitan|Q718714|WD=Q718714}}, and it became a Christian center. In {{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 20), during the {{ill|lt=Siege of Osaka#Summer Campaign|Q475780|WD=Q475780|lt=Summer Campaign of Osaka}}, the site was used as a Tokugawa main camp. After victory it came to be called '''Okachiyama''' (“victory mountain”) as an auspicious place, but most remains were destroyed and little can now be seen. 画像:忍岡古墳 01.jpg|Shinobugaoka Kofun monument 画像:忍陵神社 02.JPG|Old waka monument of Shinobu no Oka === Access === *{{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} ({{ill|lt=Katamachi Line|Q1073379|WD=Q1073379|lt=Gakkentoshi Line}}) "{{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840|WD=Q855840}}" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shinobugaoka-jinja}} *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai3sibu/sijyounawate-city/m30_06_sijyounawate_ninnryou.html Shinobugaoka Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office{{リンク切れ|date=2022-10}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しのふかおかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:四條畷市の建築物]] [[Category:四條畷市の歴史]] [[Category:藤原鎌足]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shinobugaoka-jinja, sandou.jpg|250px]]
Approach | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Shijōnawate|Q742160|WD=Q742160}} Okayama 2-7-12 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinobugaoka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinobugaoka Shrine''' (しのぶがおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Okayama, {{ill|lt=Shijōnawate|Q742160|WD=Q742160}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. The nearby {{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840|WD=Q855840}} is named after the shrine. It is sometimes read as “Ninryō-jinja.” It stands on the mound of '''{{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Kofun|Q11490749|WD=Q11490749}}''', and the site is also said to have held '''Okayama Castle''' in the Sengoku period. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995}}, with associated deities {{ill|lt=Kumano Ōkami|Q11568889|WD=Q11568889}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Umamori Ōkami}}, and {{ill|lt=Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|WD=Q167008|lt=Taishōgun-no-Kami}}. === History === Shinobugaoka Shrine was renamed in Meiji 44 after Tsuhoko Shrine merged two nearby shrines. Tsuhoko Shrine’s original deities were {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Ōkami}}. The founding date of Tsuhoko Shrine is unknown, but it is seen as originally worshiping the ancestral deity of the local ancient clan {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsuhoko clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, later taking {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} as ujigami. Until the Edo period it was called Shingū Daigongen, with a tradition that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=En no Gyōja|1=UNKNOWN}} founded it in the time of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}. As {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano worship|1=UNKNOWN}} spread in the Heian period, {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Ōkami}} was merged in. Its original site was at Akayama in Tsuboi, Okayama village, south of the current site, but in the Edo period it moved to the current site and merged Kamatari-sha (deity: Fujiwara no Kamatari) that had been here. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked village shrine of former Okayama village. In Meiji 44, it merged the following two shrines. *Former Suna village shrine, Umamori Shrine (deity {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Umamori Ōkami}}) *Former Suna village unranked shrine, Taishōgun-sha (deity {{ill|lt=Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|WD=Q167008|lt=Taishōgun-no-Kami}}) === Precincts === === Shinobugaoka Kofun === {{main|忍岡古墳}} In {{ill|lt=1934|Q18714|WD=Q18714}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 9), the shrine buildings collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Muroto Typhoon|Q4563431|WD=Q4563431}}. During restoration, a {{ill|lt=pit-type stone chamber|Q11598499|WD=Q11598499}} of a {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718}} was discovered and named “Shinobugaoka Kofun.” On March 31, {{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}, the stone chamber was designated an Osaka Prefecture {{ill|lt=cultural property|Q858308|WD=Q858308}}. Shinobugaoka Kofun is a 87 m-long keyhole-shaped tumulus built around the mid-{{ill|lt=4th century|Q8099|WD=Q8099}} in the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}}. === Okayama Castle === Okayama Castle is said to have been built around {{ill|lt=1560|Q6613|WD=Q6613}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 3), when {{ill|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Q1064909|WD=Q1064909}} held {{ill|lt=Iimoriyama Castle|Q11666638|WD=Q11666638}} as his main base, as an auxiliary castle using the ridge of Shinobugaoka Kofun northeast of Iimoriyama. In the Eiroku and {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} eras, the then-castle lord {{ill|lt=Yūki clan|Q2975399|WD=Q2975399}} were devoted {{ill|lt=Kirishitan|Q718714|WD=Q718714}}, and it became a Christian center. In {{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 20), during the {{ill|lt=Siege of Osaka#Summer Campaign|Q475780|WD=Q475780|lt=Summer Campaign of Osaka}}, the site was used as a Tokugawa main camp. After victory it came to be called '''Okachiyama''' (“victory mountain”) as an auspicious place, but most remains were destroyed and little can now be seen. 画像:忍岡古墳 01.jpg|Shinobugaoka Kofun monument 画像:忍陵神社 02.JPG|Old waka monument of Shinobu no Oka === Access === *{{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} ({{ill|lt=Katamachi Line|Q1073379|WD=Q1073379|lt=Gakkentoshi Line}}) "{{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840|WD=Q855840}}" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shinobugaoka-jinja}} *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai3sibu/sijyounawate-city/m30_06_sijyounawate_ninnryou.html Shinobugaoka Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office{{リンク切れ|date=2022-10}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しのふかおかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:四條畷市の建築物]] [[Category:四條畷市の歴史]] [[Category:藤原鎌足]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinobugaoka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinobugaoka Shrine''' (しのぶがおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Okayama, {{ill|lt=Shijōnawate|Q742160|WD=Q742160}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. The nearby {{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840|WD=Q855840}} is named after the shrine. It is sometimes read as “Ninryō-jinja.” It stands on the mound of '''{{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Kofun|Q11490749|WD=Q11490749}}''', and the site is also said to have held '''Okayama Castle''' in the Sengoku period. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995}}, with associated deities {{ill|lt=Kumano Ōkami|Q11568889|WD=Q11568889}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Umamori Ōkami}}, and {{ill|lt=Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|WD=Q167008|lt=Taishōgun-no-Kami}}. === History === Shinobugaoka Shrine was renamed in Meiji 44 after Tsuhoko Shrine merged two nearby shrines. Tsuhoko Shrine’s original deities were {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Ōkami}}. The founding date of Tsuhoko Shrine is unknown, but it is seen as originally worshiping the ancestral deity of the local ancient clan {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsuhoko clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, later taking {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} as ujigami. Until the Edo period it was called Shingū Daigongen, with a tradition that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=En no Gyōja|1=UNKNOWN}} founded it in the time of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}. As {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano worship|1=UNKNOWN}} spread in the Heian period, {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Ōkami}} was merged in. Its original site was at Akayama in Tsuboi, Okayama village, south of the current site, but in the Edo period it moved to the current site and merged Kamatari-sha (deity: Fujiwara no Kamatari) that had been here. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked village shrine of former Okayama village. In Meiji 44, it merged the following two shrines. *Former Suna village shrine, Umamori Shrine (deity {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Umamori Ōkami}}) *Former Suna village unranked shrine, Taishōgun-sha (deity {{ill|lt=Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|WD=Q167008|lt=Taishōgun-no-Kami}}) === Precincts === === Shinobugaoka Kofun === {{main|忍岡古墳}} In {{ill|lt=1934|Q18714|WD=Q18714}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 9), the shrine buildings collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Muroto Typhoon|Q4563431|WD=Q4563431}}. During restoration, a {{ill|lt=pit-type stone chamber|Q11598499|WD=Q11598499}} of a {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718}} was discovered and named “Shinobugaoka Kofun.” On March 31, {{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}, the stone chamber was designated an Osaka Prefecture {{ill|lt=cultural property|Q858308|WD=Q858308}}. Shinobugaoka Kofun is a 87 m-long keyhole-shaped tumulus built around the mid-{{ill|lt=4th century|Q8099|WD=Q8099}} in the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}}. === Okayama Castle === Okayama Castle is said to have been built around {{ill|lt=1560|Q6613|WD=Q6613}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 3), when {{ill|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Q1064909|WD=Q1064909}} held {{ill|lt=Iimoriyama Castle|Q11666638|WD=Q11666638}} as his main base, as an auxiliary castle using the ridge of Shinobugaoka Kofun northeast of Iimoriyama. In the Eiroku and {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} eras, the then-castle lord {{ill|lt=Yūki clan|Q2975399|WD=Q2975399}} were devoted {{ill|lt=Kirishitan|Q718714|WD=Q718714}}, and it became a Christian center. In {{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 20), during the {{ill|lt=Siege of Osaka#Summer Campaign|Q475780|WD=Q475780|lt=Summer Campaign of Osaka}}, the site was used as a Tokugawa main camp. After victory it came to be called '''Okachiyama''' (“victory mountain”) as an auspicious place, but most remains were destroyed and little can now be seen. 画像:忍岡古墳 01.jpg|Shinobugaoka Kofun monument 画像:忍陵神社 02.JPG|Old waka monument of Shinobu no Oka === Access === *{{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} ({{ill|lt=Katamachi Line|Q1073379|WD=Q1073379|lt=Gakkentoshi Line}}) "{{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840|WD=Q855840}}" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shinobugaoka-jinja}} *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai3sibu/sijyounawate-city/m30_06_sijyounawate_ninnryou.html Shinobugaoka Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office{{リンク切れ|date=2022-10}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しのふかおかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:四條畷市の建築物]] [[Category:四條畷市の歴史]] [[Category:藤原鎌足]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'大阪府四條畷市岡山2丁目7-12', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shinobugaoka-jinja}} [[JA:忍陵神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11490800}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2019年12月}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinobugaoka Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shinobugaoka-jinja, sandou.jpg|250px]]
Approach | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Shijōnawate|Q742160|WD=Q742160}} Okayama 2-7-12 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinobugaoka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinobugaoka Shrine''' (しのぶがおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Okayama, {{ill|lt=Shijōnawate|Q742160|WD=Q742160}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. The nearby {{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840|WD=Q855840}} is named after the shrine. It is sometimes read as “Ninryō-jinja.” It stands on the mound of '''{{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Kofun|Q11490749|WD=Q11490749}}''', and the site is also said to have held '''Okayama Castle''' in the Sengoku period. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995}}, with associated deities {{ill|lt=Kumano Ōkami|Q11568889|WD=Q11568889}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Umamori Ōkami}}, and {{ill|lt=Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|WD=Q167008|lt=Taishōgun-no-Kami}}. === History === Shinobugaoka Shrine was renamed in Meiji 44 after Tsuhoko Shrine merged two nearby shrines. Tsuhoko Shrine’s original deities were {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Ōkami}}. The founding date of Tsuhoko Shrine is unknown, but it is seen as originally worshiping the ancestral deity of the local ancient clan {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsuhoko clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, later taking {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} as ujigami. Until the Edo period it was called Shingū Daigongen, with a tradition that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=En no Gyōja|1=UNKNOWN}} founded it in the time of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}. As {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano worship|1=UNKNOWN}} spread in the Heian period, {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Ōkami}} was merged in. Its original site was at Akayama in Tsuboi, Okayama village, south of the current site, but in the Edo period it moved to the current site and merged Kamatari-sha (deity: Fujiwara no Kamatari) that had been here. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked village shrine of former Okayama village. In Meiji 44, it merged the following two shrines. *Former Suna village shrine, Umamori Shrine (deity {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Umamori Ōkami}}) *Former Suna village unranked shrine, Taishōgun-sha (deity {{ill|lt=Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|WD=Q167008|lt=Taishōgun-no-Kami}}) === Precincts === === Shinobugaoka Kofun === {{main|忍岡古墳}} In {{ill|lt=1934|Q18714|WD=Q18714}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 9), the shrine buildings collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Muroto Typhoon|Q4563431|WD=Q4563431}}. During restoration, a {{ill|lt=pit-type stone chamber|Q11598499|WD=Q11598499}} of a {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718}} was discovered and named “Shinobugaoka Kofun.” On March 31, {{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}, the stone chamber was designated an Osaka Prefecture {{ill|lt=cultural property|Q858308|WD=Q858308}}. Shinobugaoka Kofun is a 87 m-long keyhole-shaped tumulus built around the mid-{{ill|lt=4th century|Q8099|WD=Q8099}} in the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}}. === Okayama Castle === Okayama Castle is said to have been built around {{ill|lt=1560|Q6613|WD=Q6613}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 3), when {{ill|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Q1064909|WD=Q1064909}} held {{ill|lt=Iimoriyama Castle|Q11666638|WD=Q11666638}} as his main base, as an auxiliary castle using the ridge of Shinobugaoka Kofun northeast of Iimoriyama. In the Eiroku and {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} eras, the then-castle lord {{ill|lt=Yūki clan|Q2975399|WD=Q2975399}} were devoted {{ill|lt=Kirishitan|Q718714|WD=Q718714}}, and it became a Christian center. In {{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 20), during the {{ill|lt=Siege of Osaka#Summer Campaign|Q475780|WD=Q475780|lt=Summer Campaign of Osaka}}, the site was used as a Tokugawa main camp. After victory it came to be called '''Okachiyama''' (“victory mountain”) as an auspicious place, but most remains were destroyed and little can now be seen. 画像:忍岡古墳 01.jpg|Shinobugaoka Kofun monument 画像:忍陵神社 02.JPG|Old waka monument of Shinobu no Oka === Access === *{{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} ({{ill|lt=Katamachi Line|Q1073379|WD=Q1073379|lt=Gakkentoshi Line}}) "{{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840|WD=Q855840}}" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shinobugaoka-jinja}} *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai3sibu/sijyounawate-city/m30_06_sijyounawate_ninnryou.html Shinobugaoka Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office{{リンク切れ|date=2022-10}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しのふかおかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:四條畷市の建築物]] [[Category:四條畷市の歴史]] [[Category:藤原鎌足]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * しのぶがおかじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9122005001688 == Commons category (P373) == * Shinobugaoka-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府四條畷市岡山2丁目7-12', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shinobugaoka-jinja}} [[JA:忍陵神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11490800}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2019年12月}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinobugaoka Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shinobugaoka-jinja, sandou.jpg|250px]]
Approach | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Shijōnawate|Q742160|WD=Q742160}} Okayama 2-7-12 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinobugaoka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinobugaoka Shrine''' (しのぶがおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Okayama, {{ill|lt=Shijōnawate|Q742160|WD=Q742160}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. The nearby {{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840|WD=Q855840}} is named after the shrine. It is sometimes read as “Ninryō-jinja.” It stands on the mound of '''{{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Kofun|Q11490749|WD=Q11490749}}''', and the site is also said to have held '''Okayama Castle''' in the Sengoku period. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995}}, with associated deities {{ill|lt=Kumano Ōkami|Q11568889|WD=Q11568889}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Umamori Ōkami}}, and {{ill|lt=Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|WD=Q167008|lt=Taishōgun-no-Kami}}. === History === Shinobugaoka Shrine was renamed in Meiji 44 after Tsuhoko Shrine merged two nearby shrines. Tsuhoko Shrine’s original deities were {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Ōkami}}. The founding date of Tsuhoko Shrine is unknown, but it is seen as originally worshiping the ancestral deity of the local ancient clan {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsuhoko clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, later taking {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} as ujigami. Until the Edo period it was called Shingū Daigongen, with a tradition that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=En no Gyōja|1=UNKNOWN}} founded it in the time of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}. As {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano worship|1=UNKNOWN}} spread in the Heian period, {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Ōkami}} was merged in. Its original site was at Akayama in Tsuboi, Okayama village, south of the current site, but in the Edo period it moved to the current site and merged Kamatari-sha (deity: Fujiwara no Kamatari) that had been here. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked village shrine of former Okayama village. In Meiji 44, it merged the following two shrines. *Former Suna village shrine, Umamori Shrine (deity {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Umamori Ōkami}}) *Former Suna village unranked shrine, Taishōgun-sha (deity {{ill|lt=Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|WD=Q167008|lt=Taishōgun-no-Kami}}) === Precincts === === Shinobugaoka Kofun === {{main|忍岡古墳}} In {{ill|lt=1934|Q18714|WD=Q18714}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 9), the shrine buildings collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Muroto Typhoon|Q4563431|WD=Q4563431}}. During restoration, a {{ill|lt=pit-type stone chamber|Q11598499|WD=Q11598499}} of a {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718}} was discovered and named “Shinobugaoka Kofun.” On March 31, {{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}, the stone chamber was designated an Osaka Prefecture {{ill|lt=cultural property|Q858308|WD=Q858308}}. Shinobugaoka Kofun is a 87 m-long keyhole-shaped tumulus built around the mid-{{ill|lt=4th century|Q8099|WD=Q8099}} in the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}}. === Okayama Castle === Okayama Castle is said to have been built around {{ill|lt=1560|Q6613|WD=Q6613}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 3), when {{ill|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Q1064909|WD=Q1064909}} held {{ill|lt=Iimoriyama Castle|Q11666638|WD=Q11666638}} as his main base, as an auxiliary castle using the ridge of Shinobugaoka Kofun northeast of Iimoriyama. In the Eiroku and {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} eras, the then-castle lord {{ill|lt=Yūki clan|Q2975399|WD=Q2975399}} were devoted {{ill|lt=Kirishitan|Q718714|WD=Q718714}}, and it became a Christian center. In {{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 20), during the {{ill|lt=Siege of Osaka#Summer Campaign|Q475780|WD=Q475780|lt=Summer Campaign of Osaka}}, the site was used as a Tokugawa main camp. After victory it came to be called '''Okachiyama''' (“victory mountain”) as an auspicious place, but most remains were destroyed and little can now be seen. 画像:忍岡古墳 01.jpg|Shinobugaoka Kofun monument 画像:忍陵神社 02.JPG|Old waka monument of Shinobu no Oka === Access === *{{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} ({{ill|lt=Katamachi Line|Q1073379|WD=Q1073379|lt=Gakkentoshi Line}}) "{{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840|WD=Q855840}}" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shinobugaoka-jinja}} *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai3sibu/sijyounawate-city/m30_06_sijyounawate_ninnryou.html Shinobugaoka Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office{{リンク切れ|date=2022-10}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しのふかおかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:四條畷市の建築物]] [[Category:四條畷市の歴史]] [[Category:藤原鎌足]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'大阪府四條畷市岡山2丁目7-12', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shinobugaoka-jinja}} [[JA:忍陵神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11490800}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2019年12月}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinobugaoka Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shinobugaoka-jinja, sandou.jpg|250px]]
Approach | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shijōnawate|Q742160|WD=Q742160|1=Shijōnawate}} Okayama 2-7-12 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995|1=Fujiwara no Kamatari}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinobugaoka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinobugaoka Shrine''' (しのぶがおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Okayama, {{ill|lt=Shijōnawate|Q742160|WD=Q742160|1=Shijōnawate}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. The nearby {{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840|WD=Q855840|1=Shinobugaoka Station}} is named after the shrine. It is sometimes read as “Ninryō-jinja.” It stands on the mound of '''{{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Kofun|Q11490749|WD=Q11490749|1=Shinobugaoka Kofun}}''', and the site is also said to have held '''Okayama Castle''' in the Sengoku period. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995|1=Fujiwara no Kamatari}}, with associated deities {{ill|lt=Kumano Ōkami|Q11568889|WD=Q11568889|1=Kumano Okami}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Umamori Ōkami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|WD=Q167008|lt=Taishōgun-no-Kami|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)}}. === History === Shinobugaoka Shrine was renamed in Meiji 44 after Tsuhoko Shrine merged two nearby shrines. Tsuhoko Shrine’s original deities were {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995|1=Fujiwara no Kamatari}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Ōkami|1=Kumano Gongen}}. The founding date of Tsuhoko Shrine is unknown, but it is seen as originally worshiping the ancestral deity of the local ancient clan {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsuhoko clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, later taking {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} as ujigami. Until the Edo period it was called Shingū Daigongen, with a tradition that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=En no Gyōja|1=UNKNOWN}} founded it in the time of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}. As {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano worship|1=UNKNOWN}} spread in the Heian period, {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Ōkami|1=Kumano Gongen}} was merged in. Its original site was at Akayama in Tsuboi, Okayama village, south of the current site, but in the Edo period it moved to the current site and merged Kamatari-sha (deity: Fujiwara no Kamatari) that had been here. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked village shrine of former Okayama village. In Meiji 44, it merged the following two shrines. *Former Suna village shrine, Umamori Shrine (deity {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Umamori Ōkami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}) *Former Suna village unranked shrine, Taishōgun-sha (deity {{ill|lt=Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|WD=Q167008|lt=Taishōgun-no-Kami|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)}}) === Precincts === === Shinobugaoka Kofun === {{main|忍岡古墳}} In {{ill|lt=1934|Q18714|WD=Q18714|1=1934}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 9), the shrine buildings collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Muroto Typhoon|Q4563431|WD=Q4563431|1=1934 Muroto typhoon}}. During restoration, a {{ill|lt=pit-type stone chamber|Q11598499|WD=Q11598499|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} of a {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} was discovered and named “Shinobugaoka Kofun.” On March 31, {{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}}, the stone chamber was designated an Osaka Prefecture {{ill|lt=cultural property|Q858308|WD=Q858308|1=Cultural Property (Japan)}}. Shinobugaoka Kofun is a 87 m-long keyhole-shaped tumulus built around the mid-{{ill|lt=4th century|Q8099|WD=Q8099|1=4th century}} in the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}. === Okayama Castle === Okayama Castle is said to have been built around {{ill|lt=1560|Q6613|WD=Q6613|1=1560}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 3), when {{ill|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Q1064909|WD=Q1064909|1=Miyoshi Nagayoshi}} held {{ill|lt=Iimoriyama Castle|Q11666638|WD=Q11666638|1=Iimoriyama Castle}} as his main base, as an auxiliary castle using the ridge of Shinobugaoka Kofun northeast of Iimoriyama. In the Eiroku and {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} eras, the then-castle lord {{ill|lt=Yūki clan|Q2975399|WD=Q2975399|1=Yūki clan}} were devoted {{ill|lt=Kirishitan|Q718714|WD=Q718714|1=Kirishitan}}, and it became a Christian center. In {{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784|1=1615}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 20), during the {{ill|lt=Siege of Osaka#Summer Campaign|Q475780|WD=Q475780|lt=Summer Campaign of Osaka|1=Siege of Osaka}}, the site was used as a Tokugawa main camp. After victory it came to be called '''Okachiyama''' (“victory mountain”) as an auspicious place, but most remains were destroyed and little can now be seen. 画像:忍岡古墳 01.jpg|Shinobugaoka Kofun monument 画像:忍陵神社 02.JPG|Old waka monument of Shinobu no Oka === Access === *{{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} ({{ill|lt=Katamachi Line|Q1073379|WD=Q1073379|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=Katamachi Line}}) "{{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840|WD=Q855840|1=Shinobugaoka Station}}" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shinobugaoka-jinja}} *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai3sibu/sijyounawate-city/m30_06_sijyounawate_ninnryou.html Shinobugaoka Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office{{リンク切れ|date=2022-10}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しのふかおかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:四條畷市の建築物]] [[Category:四條畷市の歴史]] [[Category:藤原鎌足]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'大阪府四條畷市岡山2丁目7-12', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shinobugaoka-jinja}} [[JA:忍陵神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11490800}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2019年12月}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinobugaoka Shrine | 画像=[[File:Shinobugaoka-jinja, sandou.jpg|250px]]
Approach | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shijōnawate|Q742160|WD=Q742160|1=Shijōnawate}} Okayama 2-7-12 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995|1=Fujiwara no Kamatari}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinobugaoka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinobugaoka Shrine''' (しのぶがおかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Okayama, {{ill|lt=Shijōnawate|Q742160|WD=Q742160|1=Shijōnawate}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. The nearby {{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840|WD=Q855840|1=Shinobugaoka Station}} is named after the shrine. It is sometimes read as “Ninryō-jinja.” It stands on the mound of '''{{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Kofun|Q11490749|WD=Q11490749|1=Shinobugaoka Kofun}}''', and the site is also said to have held '''Okayama Castle''' in the Sengoku period. === Enshrined deities === The main deity is {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995|1=Fujiwara no Kamatari}}, with associated deities {{ill|lt=Kumano Ōkami|Q11568889|WD=Q11568889|1=Kumano Okami}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Umamori Ōkami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|WD=Q167008|lt=Taishōgun-no-Kami|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)}}. === History === Shinobugaoka Shrine was renamed in Meiji 44 after Tsuhoko Shrine merged two nearby shrines. Tsuhoko Shrine’s original deities were {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|Q433995|WD=Q433995|1=Fujiwara no Kamatari}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Ōkami|1=Kumano Gongen}}. The founding date of Tsuhoko Shrine is unknown, but it is seen as originally worshiping the ancestral deity of the local ancient clan {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsuhoko clan|1=UNKNOWN}}, later taking {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} as ujigami. Until the Edo period it was called Shingū Daigongen, with a tradition that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=En no Gyōja|1=UNKNOWN}} founded it in the time of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}. As {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano worship|1=UNKNOWN}} spread in the Heian period, {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Kumano Ōkami|1=Kumano Gongen}} was merged in. Its original site was at Akayama in Tsuboi, Okayama village, south of the current site, but in the Edo period it moved to the current site and merged Kamatari-sha (deity: Fujiwara no Kamatari) that had been here. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked village shrine of former Okayama village. In Meiji 44, it merged the following two shrines. *Former Suna village shrine, Umamori Shrine (deity {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Umamori Ōkami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}) *Former Suna village unranked shrine, Taishōgun-sha (deity {{ill|lt=Taishōgun (directional deity)|Q167008|WD=Q167008|lt=Taishōgun-no-Kami|1=Daishōgun (directional deity)}}) === Precincts === === Shinobugaoka Kofun === {{main|忍岡古墳}} In {{ill|lt=1934|Q18714|WD=Q18714|1=1934}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 9), the shrine buildings collapsed due to the {{ill|lt=Muroto Typhoon|Q4563431|WD=Q4563431|1=1934 Muroto typhoon}}. During restoration, a {{ill|lt=pit-type stone chamber|Q11598499|WD=Q11598499|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} of a {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} was discovered and named “Shinobugaoka Kofun.” On March 31, {{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}}, the stone chamber was designated an Osaka Prefecture {{ill|lt=cultural property|Q858308|WD=Q858308|1=Cultural Property (Japan)}}. Shinobugaoka Kofun is a 87 m-long keyhole-shaped tumulus built around the mid-{{ill|lt=4th century|Q8099|WD=Q8099|1=4th century}} in the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}}. === Okayama Castle === Okayama Castle is said to have been built around {{ill|lt=1560|Q6613|WD=Q6613|1=1560}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 3), when {{ill|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Q1064909|WD=Q1064909|1=Miyoshi Nagayoshi}} held {{ill|lt=Iimoriyama Castle|Q11666638|WD=Q11666638|1=Iimoriyama Castle}} as his main base, as an auxiliary castle using the ridge of Shinobugaoka Kofun northeast of Iimoriyama. In the Eiroku and {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} eras, the then-castle lord {{ill|lt=Yūki clan|Q2975399|WD=Q2975399|1=Yūki clan}} were devoted {{ill|lt=Kirishitan|Q718714|WD=Q718714|1=Kirishitan}}, and it became a Christian center. In {{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784|1=1615}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 20), during the {{ill|lt=Siege of Osaka#Summer Campaign|Q475780|WD=Q475780|lt=Summer Campaign of Osaka|1=Siege of Osaka}}, the site was used as a Tokugawa main camp. After victory it came to be called '''Okachiyama''' (“victory mountain”) as an auspicious place, but most remains were destroyed and little can now be seen. 画像:忍岡古墳 01.jpg|Shinobugaoka Kofun monument 画像:忍陵神社 02.JPG|Old waka monument of Shinobu no Oka === Access === *{{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} ({{ill|lt=Katamachi Line|Q1073379|WD=Q1073379|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=Katamachi Line}}) "{{ill|lt=Shinobugaoka Station|Q855840|WD=Q855840|1=Shinobugaoka Station}}" === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Shinobugaoka-jinja}} *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai3sibu/sijyounawate-city/m30_06_sijyounawate_ninnryou.html Shinobugaoka Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Office{{リンク切れ|date=2022-10}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しのふかおかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinobugaoka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:四條畷市の建築物]] [[Category:四條畷市の歴史]] [[Category:藤原鎌足]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
